news.headline: Fly Play hf. submits its financial statement in European Single Electronic Format (ESEF) format with key numbers.
Wayfair Q4 revenue beats expectations at $3.12 billion, netting $96.0 million EBITDA, yet shares fall on high ad spend.
news.summary: Stocks of Wayfair Inc (NYSE:W) dropped on Friday despite the company surpassing Q4 revenue expectations on Thursday. Analyst Mark Kelley from Stifel reiterated a Hold rating, lowering the price target to $46 from $47. The company's net revenue of $3.12 billion, although flat year-over-year, exceeded Street forecasts of $3.07 billion. Active customers totaled 21.4 million, below the anticipated 22.2 million, with adjusted EBITDA of $96.0 million missing the expected $105.1 million. The earnings shortfall was attributed to higher ad spend compared to the previous quarter. Conversely, analyst Bobby Griffin from Raymond James upheld a Strong Buy rating with a price target of $60. Adjusted EBITDA for Wayfair fell short of expectations by approximately $10 million.

news.headline: Best Contracting Services named Regional Contractor of the Year by Tremco at the 2025 IRE in San Antonio for outstanding performance in roofing industry.
news.summary: In San Antonio, Best Contracting Services, a top provider of roofing and building envelope solutions, was honored as the Regional Contractor of the Year by Tremco Incorporated at the 2025 International Roofing Expo (IRE) in Texas. This accolade celebrates Best Contracting Services' dedication to quality, creativity, and exceptional performance within the commercial roofing sector. This marks the second industry recognition for the company.

news.headline: Gold prices continue rising as investors seek its safe haven appeal during periods of uncertainty.
news.summary: Investors typically turn to gold, a valuable commodity known for its stability, as a safe haven during periods of uncertainty or crisis.

news.headline: Global anti-drone market valued at USD 2.71 billion in 2024, projected to reach USD 11.12 billion by 2030 with 26.53% CAGR.
news.summary: In Dublin, the Anti-drone Market report, available on ResearchAndMarkets.com, predicts significant growth. The market was worth USD 2.71 billion in 2024 and is forecasted to hit USD 11.12 billion by 2030, showing a notable CAGR of 26.53%. The global landscape for anti-drone technology has become fiercely competitive, with key players such as Raytheon Technologies and Lockheed Martin leading the market.

news.headline: Global elemental phosphorus (P4) market grows at 5.7% CAGR, expected to reach USD 4.6 billion by 2034. Demand driven by agriculture, energy storage, and industry applications. Key players include Israel Chemicals Limited and Bayer AG, leveraging phosphorus for specialty chemicals and crop protection products.
news.summary: The global elemental phosphorus (P4) and derivatives market was valued at US$2.5 billion in 2023 and is projected to reach US$4.6 billion by 2034, with a CAGR of 5.7% from 2024 to 2034. This growth is driven by demand from agriculture, advancements in energy storage, and expanding applications in various industries. Elemental phosphorus is used in fertilizers, flame retardants, water treatment, and lithium-ion batteries for electric vehicles. Key players in the market include Israel Chemicals Limited, Bayer AG, and Clariant AG.

news.headline: Bybit exchange breach: $1.4B stETH stolen in cold wallet hack on Feb. 21.
news.summary: On February 21st, a cybercriminal successfully gained access to an Ethereum cold wallet on the Bybit platform, absconding with more than $1.4 billion in assets.

news.headline: Black Sea CPC Blend Oil exports revised up for February to 1.67 mln bpd vs 1.42 mln bpd in the previous plan - Sources.

Telephone and Data Systems reports 5.6% decline in Q4 revenue to $1.240 billion, UScellular revenue drops 3% to $970M, TDS Telecom segment revenues rise by 1%, residential broadband connections grow by 2.4%. Stock value dips after earnings announcement.
news.summary: Telephone and Data Systems, Inc. (NYSE:TDS) saw a drop in shares following the release of their fourth-quarter FY24 earnings report. The company experienced a 5.6% year-on-year decline in fourth-quarter revenue, totaling $1.240 billion, below the anticipated analyst consensus of $1.254 billion. Despite this, the adjusted EPS loss of 9 cents surpassed expectations of a 28 cent loss. UScellular revealed a 3% revenue decrease to $970 million, with Postpaid Average Revenue Per User (ARPU) growing by 0.2% to $51.73. Conversely, TDS Telecom segment revenues increased by 1% to $264 million, driven by a 2.4% growth in residential broadband connections.

news.headline: OhmX RAMP UP Grant awarded to 15 labs for genomic research advancement by Nabsys and Hitachi High-Tech.
news.summary: In Providence, Rhode Island, on February 21, 2025, Nabsys 2.0, LLC (Nabsys) and Hitachi High-Tech America, Inc. (HTA) announced the recipients of the OhmX RAMP UP Grant Program. This initiative aims at accelerating market access for innovative solutions in structural analysis. The partnership will support a select number of applicants to bring their products to market swiftly.

news.headline: Market plunges by 400 points on low consumer sentiment reading.
Rivian beats estimates, earnings aided by sales of credits/software, stock tests key level after loss.
news.summary: Rivian's earnings surpassed estimates, largely driven by the sale of regulatory credits and software. The company's stock is currently testing a crucial level following a loss that was not as severe as anticipated.

news.headline: Hims stock drops after FDA resolves Novo Nordisk weight-loss drug shortage. - Investor's Business Daily.
news.summary: Following the FDA announcement that the scarcity of Novo Nordisk's weight-loss medication has been rectified, HIMS stock experienced a decline. The FDA's resolution of the shortage had a direct impact on HIMS stock, leading to a drop in its value. This development was covered in an article titled "Hims Stock Crashes After FDA Resolves Weight-Loss Drugs Shortage."

news.headline: CBD products can aid in New Year's resolutions, with 80% of Americans aiming for a healthier lifestyle.
news.summary: In Miami on February 21, 2025, a report stated that approximately 80% of individuals in America aim to adopt a healthier and more active lifestyle this year. To support this goal, considering Fusion CBD Products such as CBD Intensive Relief Rub, Pure CBD Capsules designed for muscle and joint relief, or Slim Fast could be beneficial.

news.headline: Fla. housing market in Jan 2025 shows rise in new listings & inventory compared to the previous year.
news.summary: In February 2025, Orlando, Florida saw a rise in new listings and inventory in the housing market compared to the previous year. According to the latest data from Florida Realtors®, there has been an increase in active listings. Despite this positive trend, home sales continue to be impacted by certain factors. The market is showing promising signs with this growth in listings and inventory, indicating potential for increased activity in the real estate sector.

news.headline: Hacker breaches Bybit exchange, moving 1.4 billion worth of ETH after gaining control of a cold wallet on Feb. 21.
news.summary: On February 21st, a hacker gained access to an Ethereum cold wallet located on the Bybit exchange, managing to steal funds totaling more than $1.4 billion.

news.headline: Awardco ranked #20 in G2's Best Software Products of 2025, among over 13,000 eligible companies globally.
news.summary: Awardco, a company specializing in employee rewards and recognition, has achieved the impressive ranking of #20 out of more than 13,000 eligible software companies on a global scale. Based in Provo, Utah, Awardco disclosed this achievement on February 21, 2025, via a PRNewswire report. The company has been recognized as one of G2's Top 100 Best Software Products.

news.headline: Icelandic Competition Authority approved Landsbankinn's acquisition of TM tryggingar with condition. Settlement with Kvika Bank on 28 Feb 2025.
news.summary: The acquisition of all share capital in TM tryggingar hf. by Landsbankinn has been approved by the Icelandic Competition Authority, with a condition stipulated in a settlement between the Bank and the Authority. As per the agreement, Landsbankinn commits to ensuring that specific insurance terms from TM will not require customers to have their salaries deposited into a Bank account. This condition meets the regulatory criteria for finalizing the purchase. Landsbankinn anticipates taking over ownership of TM after resolving matters with Kvika Bank hf. by February 28, 2025.

news.headline: $1.4 billion stolen from Bybit in a hack confirmed by CEO, resulting in Ethereum drop.
news.summary: On Friday, CEO Ben Zhou of the crypto exchange Bybit confirmed that the platform had been attacked, resulting in the apparent theft of over $1.4 billion worth of funds.

news.headline: Thailand's BNPL market to hit $6.6 billion by 2030 with key players like Atome, K PAY LATER, SpayLater, and Pay Next.
news.summary: A report on the Thailand Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) market has been released, offering insights into business and investment opportunities. The BNPL payment market in Thailand is projected to expand by 14.9% annually, reaching an estimated value of US$3.94 billion by 2025. This market saw significant growth between 2021 and 2024, with a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of ____%. The report provides over 75 key performance indicators (KPIs) on market size, end-use sectors, market share, product analysis, business model, and demographics, with data updated as of Q1 2025.

news.headline: Morgan "Mac" Schuessler joins Deluxe Board of Directors - brings 30+ years experience in payments. CEO of Evertec Group.
news.summary: Deluxe, a leading Payments and Data company listed on the NYSE under the ticker symbol DLX, recently appointed Morgan M. “Mac” Schuessler to their Board of Directors. With over 30 years of expertise in payments and financial technology sectors, Mr. Schuessler presently holds the titles of Chief Executive Officer, President, and director at Evertec Group, a role he has maintained since 2015. Prior to this, he was the President, International, at Global Payments.

news.headline: Keysight will showcase wireless innovations at Mobile World Congress 2025, featuring AI and secure networks to achieve breakthroughs in 5G and 6G research. Characters: 130
news.summary: Keysight (NYSE: KEYS) will demonstrate the latest advancements in wireless technology at Mobile World Congress 2025. The event will take place from March 3-6, 2025, in Hall 5. By leveraging artificial intelligence (AI) and ensuring the security of devices and networks, Keysight is at the forefront of 5G and 6G research. Their comprehensive wireless portfolio enables innovative use cases, minimizes risks, and accelerates time to market, cementing their position as a pioneer in the industry.

news.headline: Candace Klein, WBE certified, forms alliance with 84 Lumber, driving success in construction. Women In Construction Week, March 2-8, 2025.
news.summary: From March 2 to 8, 2025, Women In Construction Week will be celebrated in Eighty Four, Pennsylvania. Despite being a male-dominated industry associated with hard hats and heavy machinery, Candace Klein is challenging stereotypes as the president of Klein Contracting Corporation. This event aims to highlight the contributions of women in construction.

news.headline: NEB to showcase advanced NGS sample prep innovations at AGBT 2025, including fast library prep and new epigenetics tech.
news.summary: Information about cutting-edge technologies, such as efficient library preparation methods and advancements in epigenetics, will be revealed at an upcoming event in Ipswich, Massachusetts on February 21, 2025. New England Biolabs (NEB®) will showcase a variety of new sequencing platforms and innovations. Stay tuned for further details on these groundbreaking technologies.

news.headline: NAVER D2SF invests in whereable.ai, an AI-powered autonomous driving startup, securing Incheon Airport project in 4 months.
news.summary: A newly developed plug-and-play autonomous driving solution allows for immediate operation in various environments. The initiative was launched by the founders of ThorDrive, a prominent autonomous vehicle startup. Impressively, the project secured a major deal at Incheon Airport in just four months post its inception. NAVER D2SF has pledged to support ongoing collaborations to further enhance the technology and its applications.

news.headline: Zions Bancorporation's President to speak at RBC Capital Markets Conference on March 4 at 4 pm Eastern.
news.summary: On February 21, 2025, Scott McLean, the President and Chief Operating Officer of Zions Bancorporation, N.A. (NASDAQ: ZION), is scheduled to speak at the RBC Capital Markets Global Financial Institutions Conference on March 4th at 4:00 pm Eastern Time. The conference will feature an audio webcast of the presentation.

news.headline: Approval granted for Landsbankinn's acquisition of TM tryggingar from Kvika bank, transfer set for Feb 28.
news.summary: Competition Authority has confirmed a resolution with Landsbankinn on the purchase of all TM tryggingar’s shares from Kvika bank. This eliminates the requirement for Central Bank of Iceland’s Financial Supervisory Authority and Competition Authority approvals as per the agreement's terms. Landsbankinn is set to take over the insurance company on February 28 and will compensate Kvika bank with the specified acquisition cost.

news.headline: StoneX Group CEO discusses gold market volatility due to Trump's tariff policies on Sky News Arabia.
news.summary: On February 21, 2025, in London, Philip Smith, Chief Executive of StoneX Group, discussed concerns related to Trump's tariffs and gold market volatility on Sky News Arabia. This discussion was timely as uncertainties loom, impacting physical gold flows and pricing disparities. Smith's insights shed light on these issues, highlighting the need for attention to the market's fluctuations and policy changes. This segment aimed to inform viewers about the ongoing challenges in the market, with a focus on addressing these issues to navigate the current economic landscape effectively.

news.headline: Ferrari releases 2024 Annual Report and files Form 20-F with SEC; includes Sustainability Statement as per ESRS.
news.summary: On February 21, 2025, Ferrari N.V. disclosed the release of its 2024 Annual Report and submission to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission of Form 20-F containing financial data for the fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2024. Noteworthy to mention is the debut of the 2024 Sustainability Statement, developed per the European Sustainability Reporting Standard (ESRS) guidelines on a voluntary basis, integrated within the 2024 Annual Report.

news.headline: European Defense Chiefs are to hold an emergency meeting on Ukraine.
JCP&L is upgrading power system in Mercer County, benefiting nearly 1,000 customers. Byline: Holmdel, N.J., Feb. 21, 2025.
news.summary: Around 1,000 customers are set to gain from the initiative in HOLMDEL, N.J., as of February 21, 2025. Jersey Central Power & Light (JCP&L), a branch of FirstEnergy Corp. (NYSE: FE), is enhancing its power grid in sections of Mercer County to reduce the occurrence and severity of disruptions.

news.headline: PMI refutes Stanley Black & Decker's lawsuit over "Stanley" brand ownership in food/beverage containers.
news.summary: PMI is the owner of the "Stanley" brand for the products it is currently marketing, holding the trademark for both "Stanley" and the winged bear logo in the food and beverage container sector. A lawsuit filed by Stanley Black & Decker aims to leverage PMI's accomplishments with the Stanley brand.

news.headline: US stock futures fall; inflation data awaited. CPI forecasted at 2.9% YoY, 0.3% MoM. 10-year Treasury yield at 4.54%. Dow down 0.17%, Nasdaq down 0.07%. SPY at $604.45, QQQ at $527.67. Market reacted mixed to tariff announcements and Powell's testimony.
news.summary: U.S. stock futures declined on Wednesday as Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell testified before Congress. Economists predict a 2.9% year-over-year consumer price index with a 0.3% monthly increase in January. The 10-year Treasury yield was at 4.54%, and the two-year yield at 4.29%. There is a 95.5% chance of no interest rate changes in the March Fed meeting. Benchmark indices were down except for the Russell 2000. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust fell by 0.14% to $604.45, while the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF dropped 0.06% to $527.67. In the last session, Tesla shares dropped, DoorDash surged, Marriott tumbled, and Coca-Cola rose. Powell stated no urgency to shift interest rates and refrained from discussing trade policy. Communication services, health care, and consumer discretionary sectors fell, while others rose. The Dow closed at 44,593.65, up 0.28%.

news.headline: President Trump shifts stance on inflation, now concedes prices may take longer to reduce after promising to lower them immediately.
news.summary: During his campaign, President Trump promised to reduce costs from the beginning. However, his administration has admitted that achieving this goal will require more time than initially anticipated.

news.headline: Discover the booming export industry in Guangzhou, producing cheap goods flooding global markets tariff-free, along the Pearl River.
news.summary: In Southern China, a rapidly expanding industry along the Pearl River is producing affordable clothing and toys, which are being exported worldwide without duties.

news.headline: Inflation surges past expectations in January: CPI rises to 3% year-over-year, monthly growth at 0.5%. Core inflation increases to 3.3%.
news.summary: Price pressures rose more than anticipated in the first month of the year, driven by increases in energy goods and service items, impacting investor outlook on potential Federal Reserve interest rate adjustments. The headline Consumer Price Index climbed to 3% year-over-year, exceeding median economist predictions of 2.9%. Monthly inflation also surpassed expectations, increasing by 0.5% compared to the anticipated 0.3%. This rise in inflation is the most significant since August 2023. Core inflation, which excludes volatile food and energy prices, rose from 3.2% to 3.3% year-over-year, with a monthly increase of 0.4%.

news.headline: Euro tariffs on American whiskey set to snap back on March 31 unless extended, highlighting impact of trade war.
news.summary: As the world approaches another trade dispute, European tariffs on American whiskey are set to increase after March 31 unless an extension is approved. This means that the liquor industry may face challenges as tariffs snap back into place, impacting the trade relationship between Europe and the US. This decision could have significant consequences for the American whiskey market in terms of sales and revenue.

news.headline: G-20 displays unity amid global order turmoil sparked by Trump's actions.
Company anticipates a loss in the first half due to impairment charges. Details to be revealed. #BusinessNews
President Trump intensifies tariff threats on global scale, potentially disrupting global trade with actions surpassing those in his first term.
news.summary: In just under a month as president, the current administration has already implemented trade measures that have the potential to significantly disrupt the global trading system. These recent actions far surpass in magnitude the trade policies enacted throughout the entirety of the president's first term in office.

news.headline: Inflation data surpass expectations: CPI up 3%, fuel oil rose 6.2%, Fed rate cut expectations pushed to 2025 and 2026.
news.summary: The January inflation report showed a higher-than-expected increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI), with a 3.0% year-over-year rise, exceeding the prior reading and expectations of 2.9%. This marked the fourth consecutive annual inflation increase, with a significant monthly increase of 0.5%, the largest since August 2023. Fuel oil had the biggest jump at 6.2% month-over-month, while the index for meats, poultry, fish, and eggs rose 1.9%, including a 15.2% increase for eggs. Following this report, traders adjusted their expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts, pushing the first anticipated cut to December 2025 and the second to no earlier than September 2026. This shift is also reflected in the betting odds on the CFTC-regulated Kalshi platform, where a single rate cut in 2025 is seen as the most likely scenario.

news.headline: Verizon will redeem debt securities on March 18, 2025, as announced on Feb. 14, 2025.
news.summary: Verizon Communications Inc. (NYSE, NASDAQ: VZ) disclosed plans to redeem certain notes on March 18, 2025. The redemption date was confirmed on February 14, 2025. Verizon did not specify the exact value or quantity of the notes being redeemed in the announcement.

news.headline: THEON's FY 2024 preliminary results exceed guidance, signaling a prosperous year with expectations of continued growth in FY 2025.
UnitedHealth is under DOJ investigation for medical billing practices, according to Bloomberg.
Trump criticizes Europe's VAT system, saying it gives unfair trade advantages. Here's how it began.
news.summary: The president has expressed concerns about the Value-Added Tax (VAT) system utilized in Europe, arguing that it creates a trade disparity benefiting other nations. The VAT system was initially implemented to help bolster revenues in the 1960s, evolving into a key source of income for various European countries. However, the president believes this system now poses challenges for U.S. businesses, with VAT rates averaging around 21% across Europe. This issue has sparked discussions about potential reforms to address the perceived trade advantages enjoyed by other countries.

news.headline: Global Policy Advisors predict Federal Reserve involvement in proposed U.S. sovereign wealth fund.
news.summary: Salar Ghahramani, a Global Policy Advisor, has predicted that the Federal Reserve will play a significant role in a planned sovereign wealth fund for the United States.

news.headline: January's CPI rose to 3% YoY, highest since August 2023, driven by food and energy prices. Experts warn of inflation impact on future interest rates.
news.summary: Inflation data for January surprised experts by rising to 3% year-over-year, surpassing expectations. Monthly inflation saw its highest increase since August 2023, attributed to surges in food and energy prices. Additionally, core inflation, excluding these factors, also rose, indicating broader concerns. Senior investment strategist Charlie Ripley from Allianz Investment Management noted that potential price hikes due to President Donald Trump's tariffs haven't been factored in yet. The market may need to reassess its outlook on interest rates in light of these developments. For more information, visit the full story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Elon Musk visits Dept. of Labor, could impact inflation trends significantly.
news.summary: In the latest data release, it was reported that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 5.5%, marking a significant decrease from the previous month. The economy also added 250,000 new jobs, exceeding expectations and indicating a positive trend in the job market. These numbers reflect a steady recovery from the economic downturn and offer hope for continued growth in the coming months. Additionally, the report highlighted a 2.3% increase in average hourly wages, providing further evidence of improving conditions for workers. Overall, the data paints a promising picture of recovery and resilience in the economy.

news.headline: Abbott declares 405th consecutive quarterly dividend - 59 cents per share, paid since 1924.
news.summary: Abbott, based in Abbott Park, Illinois, announced on February 21, 2025, that its board of directors approved a quarterly common dividend of 59 cents per share. This dividend payment, set to be distributed on May 15, 2025, will be the company's 405th consecutive quarterly dividend since 1924. Abbott, listed on the New York Stock Exchange under the ticker symbol ABT, has shown a strong commitment to returning value to its shareholders through consistent dividend payouts for over a century.

news.headline: DOGE gains traction in Washington as Elon Musk leads efforts to revamp government and cut spending. Wall Street not impressed.
news.summary: After only a brief period since the beginning of the DOGE initiative, Elon Musk has made numerous bold statements regarding the project. He is spearheading an effort to bring significant changes to the federal government, reduce supervision of private businesses, and reduce expenditures.
